diagrams.title: Basic Turing Machine Structure
diagrams.description: Shows the core components: infinite tape divided into cells, read/write head, and finite control unit with state register
diagrams.purpose: Understanding TM architecture and how components interact during computation

diagrams.title: Transition Function Representation
diagrams.description: Illustrates how a TM transition δ(q, X) = (p, Y, D) works - reading symbol X in state q, writing Y, moving direction D, transitioning to state p
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the step-by-step operation of TM computation

diagrams.title: Church-Turing Thesis Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Shows the inclusion hierarchy: Regular languages ⊂ Context-free ⊂ Context-sensitive ⊂ Recursively enumerable ⊂ All possible strings
elements: Examples of languages in each class
diagrams.purpose: Understanding computational power hierarchy and what TM can compute vs other models

diagrams.title: Halting Problem Proof Diagram
diagrams.description: Visual representation of Turing's diagonalization proof showing the contradiction in assuming a Halting Problem decider exists
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the fundamental limits of computation through visual proof structure

visuals.title: Introduction To Turing Machines
visuals.description: Visual diagram for introduction to turing machines
